operations: Parse CLI flags: --run-id, --count, --repo, --model, --models, --metaswarm, --agent-cmd, --teardown, --no-attach, --dry-run, --preflight
Validate run_id format matches ^run-[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}$
Validate worker_count >= 1 and integer
Metaswarm cap: max 5 commanders
Verify --repo is a git repository
Metaswarm model policy: ban cheap models (haiku-4.5, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-4.1), default to premium rotation if --model/--models not set
protocol_phases.details: Pure validation phase. Sets STAMPEDE_BASE=$REPO_PATH/.stampede, BASE_DIR=$STAMPEDE_BASE/$RUN_ID, SESSION_NAME=stampede-$RUN_ID, PIDS_DIR=$BASE_DIR/pids. Run directory must already exist (created by orchestrator skill).

operations: check_prereqs(): verify required bins (tmux, python3, jq, openssl, git, bash)
Check optional bins (watch, copilot, gh)
Copilot CLI fallback: copilot -> gh copilot extension -> warn
Custom agent cmd skips Copilot check
protocol_phases.details: Landmine #22 awareness. Fails hard if required binaries missing. Optional bins degrade gracefully (monitor pane skipped).

operations: mkdir -p $PIDS_DIR (pids/)
mkdir -p $BASE_DIR/scripts
mkdir -p $BASE_DIR/logs
If metaswarm: mkdir -p $BASE_DIR/commanders
If metaswarm: mkdir -p $BASE_DIR/collab
protocol_phases.details: CRITICAL: The launcher does NOT create queue/, claimed/, or results/ directories. It expects them pre-created (by the orchestrator skill). It only creates operational dirs: pids, scripts, logs. Line 602 exits with error if BASE_DIR doesn't exist; line 607 exits if queue has 0 tasks.

operations: tmux has-session -t $SESSION_NAME check
If exists: kill-session then sleep 1
protocol_phases.details: Landmine #12 prevention. Ensures no stale tmux session conflicts with the new launch.

operations: Parse MODELS (or fallback to MODEL) into MODEL_LIST array
MODEL_COUNT = array length
get_worker_model() assigns model via (worker_num - 1) % MODEL_COUNT
protocol_phases.details: Round-robin model assignment across workers/commanders. Metaswarm default is 10-model premium rotation.

operations: Python3 script writes fleet.json with per-worker/commander config
Normal mode: fleet entries have {model, slot, role: 'worker'}
Metaswarm: creates commanders/{cid}/atoms/ and commanders/{cid}/logs/ per commander
Metaswarm: writes swarm-state.json per commander (initial phase=commander_pane_starting, status=starting)
Metaswarm: writes child-agents.jsonl per commander (first event: commander_registered)
Metaswarm collab bus: creates collab/ dir with protocol.json and 5 empty JSONL ledgers
Metaswarm: updates state.json with profile=metaswarm and collab reference
protocol_phases.details: The collaboration bus is a set of append-only JSONL ledgers under collab/. Protocol.json defines version 1.0, ledger paths, atomic append guidance (O_APPEND semantics), commander workflow sequence (propose -> peer_review -> improve -> consensus -> broadcast -> adopt), and required fields (ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, evidence, confidence, source_refs). Each commander's fleet entry includes full swarm config: ss-250, 50 squad leads, 5 workers per lead, premium model pool, banned models, depth budget, and telemetry contract paths.

operations: run_default_agent_launch_preflight(): spawns a probe tmux session
Generates copilot-preflight.sh that asks agent to reply STAMPEDE_PREFLIGHT_OK
Wraps in copilot-preflight-runner.sh for exit code capture
Waits up to COPILOT_PREFLIGHT_TIMEOUT (default 60s)
On failure: calls mark_run_blocked('auth_blocked', detail) which updates state.json and all commander swarm-state.json files
protocol_phases.details: Skipped if --agent-cmd is set. Uses the target agent role (stampede-agent for normal, stampede-commander for metaswarm) and first model in rotation. Kills probe session after test. Redacts tokens >30 chars in error output.

operations: build_worker_script() per worker/commander
Generates $BASE_DIR/scripts/agent-N.sh (executable)
Sets env vars: STAMPEDE_RUN_ID, STAMPEDE_RUN_DIR, STAMPEDE_ROLE, STAMPEDE_AGENT_LOG
Metaswarm adds: STAMPEDE_COMMANDER_ID, STAMPEDE_PROGRESS_DIR, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_PROTOCOL, and all 5 ledger env vars
Normal: uses --agent stampede-agent with task-claiming prompt
Metaswarm: uses --agent stampede-commander with full swarm deployment prompt including collaboration bus instructions
Custom agent-cmd: substitutes {prompt} and {model} placeholders
Default Copilot: copilot --agent --model --allow-all-tools --autopilot --max-autopilot-continues N --no-ask-user -p PROMPT | tee log
On commander failure: python3 updates swarm-state.json to failed and appends commander_launch_failed to child-agents.jsonl
Ends with sleep 86400 to keep pane alive for debugging
protocol_phases.details: The prompt embeds the full operational contract. Metaswarm commander prompt is ~500 words covering: task claiming, swarm config (ss-250, 50x5=250), model policy, telemetry contract, collaboration bus protocol with all ledgers and workflow, and bundle output requirements. STAMPEDE_METASWARM_NO_GATES env var disables timeouts/circuit-breakers.

operations: tmux new-session -d -s $SESSION_NAME -x 220 -y 50 <first_cmd>
Set remain-on-exit on (crashed panes stay visible)
Monitor pane gets slot 0 if stampede-monitor.sh exists or watch is available
If no monitor: first worker script becomes pane 0
protocol_phases.details: Session dimensions 220x50. Monitor precedence: stampede-monitor.sh > watch fallback > no monitor (worker takes pane 0).

operations: Loop from START_INDEX to WORKER_COUNT
tmux split-window -t $SESSION_NAME <worker_script>
tmux select-layout tiled after each split
sleep 1 between spawns (stabilization)
protocol_phases.details: START_INDEX=1 if monitor occupies pane 0, else 2 (worker 1 already in pane 0). Each pane runs its own agent-N.sh script.

operations: Set pane-border-status top, border styles (cyan active, grey inactive)
Set pane-border-format with lightning emoji and pane_title
Label monitor pane as '📊 Monitor'
Label worker panes as 'Agent #N · model · task_title'
Label commander panes as 'Commander #N · model · full SS-250'
protocol_phases.details: Task titles read from queue JSON files for pane labels. Provides visual identification of what each pane is working on.

operations: sleep 5 (wait for processes to start)
tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_index} #{pane_pid}'
find_leaf_pid() walks process tree looking for copilot/gh/claude/aider processes
Write leaf PID to $PIDS_DIR/worker-N.pid or commander-NNN.pid
protocol_phases.details: Landmine #16: pane PID != worker PID. find_leaf_pid walks children via pgrep -P, matching command names containing 'copilot', 'gh copilot', 'claude', or 'aider'. Falls through to deepest child if no match found.

operations: Verify each PID is alive via kill -0
Print launched/total count
Print tmux attach and teardown commands
Auto-attach: on macOS opens Terminal.app with ASCII art splash, progress bar, then tmux attach
protocol_phases.details: Auto-attach suppressed by --no-attach (used by orchestrator skill). macOS splash includes sound effect (Blow.aiff) and fullscreen attempt.

metaswarm_differences.metaswarm: Hard cap of 5 commanders (each runs 250 sub-agents = 1250 max, doc says 1600 cap)
Premium-only enforced. Banned models cause exit 1. Default is 10-model premium rotation.
Additionally creates commanders/, collab/, commanders/{cid}/atoms/, commanders/{cid}/logs/
Full collab bus: protocol.json + 5 append-only JSONL ledgers (proposals, reviews, improvements, consensus, broadcasts) with O_APPEND semantics and workflow sequence.
stampede-commander (can use task tool, spawns sub-swarms)
Each commander must maintain swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl with launch proof. Fleet.json includes telemetry_contract with file paths and launch_proof_required=true.

metaswarm_differences.normal: Agent script exits with status code, no special handling
metaswarm_differences.metaswarm: On Copilot launch failure: python3 updates swarm-state.json to failed state and appends commander_launch_failed event to child-agents.jsonl with redacted log tail

metaswarm_differences.normal: ~50 words: claim task, complete, write result
metaswarm_differences.metaswarm: ~500 words: full swarm config, model policy, telemetry contract, collaboration bus protocol, bundle output requirements
All normal vars plus STAMPEDE_COMMANDER_ID, STAMPEDE_PROGRESS_DIR, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_PROTOCOL, and 5 individual ledger vars
STAMPEDE_METASWARM_NO_GATES=1 disables timeouts, circuit breakers, and cost ceilings for commanders

issues.detail: The launcher assumes BASE_DIR and queue/ already exist (lines 602-609 exit if missing). Documentation says 'queue, claimed, results, logs, pids' but the launcher only creates pids, scripts, logs. The orchestrator skill must create queue/claimed/results. This is correct separation of concerns but underdocumented in the launcher itself.

issues.issue: fleet.json written non-atomically in normal mode
issues.detail: In normal mode, fleet.json is written directly via open(path, 'w'). In metaswarm mode, write_json_atomic (tmp+replace) is used for state.json and protocol.json but fleet.json at line 863 still uses direct write. Not a real race risk since fleet.json is written before agents launch, but inconsistent with the atomic-write convention.

If no process matching copilot/claude/aider is found in the tree, find_leaf_pid returns the input pane_pid itself (line 87). This means PID files may contain shell PIDs rather than agent PIDs if the agent hasn't started within the 5s sleep window.

Line 505 says 'total cap 1600' but 5 commanders × 250 workers = 1250. The 1600 figure is undocumented. Possibly accounts for squad leads (5×50=250 squad leads + 1250 workers = 1500) plus 5 commanders + monitor overhead, but it's not clearly derived.

The fleet.json Python block updates state.json with collab info (line 730-740) but only if state.json already exists. If the orchestrator skill hasn't written state.json before launcher runs, the collab reference won't be in state.json. Silently skipped via try/except.

Worker scripts end with 'sleep 86400' (24h) to keep panes alive after completion. Combined with remain-on-exit, dead panes are preserved for debugging but consume tmux resources indefinitely if teardown isn't called.

summary: The stampede.sh launcher implements a 12-phase protocol: arg parsing → prereq check → directory scaffolding → duplicate session guard → model rotation → fleet.json + collab bus init → Copilot launch preflight → worker script generation → tmux session creation → pane spawning → pane labeling → PID capture + status. The launcher does NOT create the task queue (queue/claimed/results); it expects these pre-created by the orchestrator skill. In metaswarm mode, it additionally creates the collaboration bus (collab/ with protocol.json + 5 JSONL ledgers), per-commander progress dirs with swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl telemetry, and injects a rich 500-word prompt with full swarm config, premium model policy, and collaboration workflow instructions. PID tracking uses process tree walking (Landmine #16) to find the actual agent process rather than the shell pane PID. The protocol is well-structured with clear phase separation, though fleet.json is written non-atomically and the 1600-cap figure is undocumented.
